description:
You are collaborating with a colleague who maintains dozens of small Python utilities in
    /home/user/projects.  
    Your colleague wants a plain-text inventory of just the **top-level** “.py” files that live
    directly in that directory (not in any sub-directories).  
    The inventory must be written to a new file called

        /home/user/projects/python_file_list.txt

    Requirements for python_file_list.txt that the automated test will enforce:

    1. Content  
       • Each line must contain **only** the file’s base name (e.g. “example.py”),  
         without any preceding path information.  
       • List every “*.py” file that is located directly inside /home/user/projects.  
       • Do **not** list files from subdirectories.  
       • Sort the lines in strictly alphanumeric order (the same order produced by the “sort”
         command’s default behavior).  
       • End the file with a single trailing newline character after the last filename.

    2. Location & permissions  
       • The file must be created exactly at /home/user/projects/python_file_list.txt.  
       • Leave the default permissions that the shell assigns when redirecting output.

    3. Method constraint  
       • Achieve the result with one compound or piped command entered on a single terminal
         line (i.e. no intermediate temporary files).

    After running your command, you should see the newly created file present and its contents
    must match the specification above.  The test harness will open that exact file and compare
    it byte-for-byte with the expected result, so pay close attention to sorting and the final
    newline.

    Perform the task entirely from the terminal.  You do NOT need to supply the command here—
    just run it so the state of the filesystem meets the requirements.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts:

    Directory /home/user/projects/ already exists and contains the following regular files
    (all plain UTF-8 text):

        alpha.py               # sample Python file (content irrelevant)
        beta.py                # sample Python file
        gamma_processor.py     # sample Python file
        zeta.py                # sample Python file
        readme.md              # non-Python file
        data.csv               # non-Python file

    It also contains a subdirectory /home/user/projects/old/ which itself contains
        legacy.py

    Verification criteria for the grader:

    1. The agent must create /home/user/projects/python_file_list.txt.
    2. The file’s exact contents must be:

        alpha.py
        beta.py
        gamma_processor.py
        zeta.py

        …with a single trailing newline after “zeta.py”.
    3. No other changes to the directory structure or file contents are required.
